currentPath.summary: Discovery-first product and business build: turn planning into publishable site structure, clearer command surfaces, and a working launch flow while keeping autonomous activity visible.
focus: Turn founder answers into durable planning docs.
Translate planning into public-facing messaging and page structure.
Make the dashboard a clearer command surface instead of a long scrolling log.
Keep heartbeat/autonomous activity visible enough that Kirill can tell what is running and why.

nextSteps.title: Turn Start Here into a first simple prototype
nextSteps.why: The launch checklist now exists, so the next useful move is converting the routing plan into a concrete page or section draft.

nextSteps.title: Add heartbeat/activity visibility to the live dashboard UI
nextSteps.why: Kirill wants to see what runs, how often, and what the assistant is doing without digging through chat.

nextSteps.title: Recommend the first concrete launch asset
nextSteps.why: The next product decision is still workbook vs. unit study vs. podcast/blog-first sequencing.

nextSteps.title: Continue direct email discovery only for tactical follow-up questions
nextSteps.why: Broad discovery improved a lot; remaining questions are now narrower and sequencing-oriented.

completed: Created and published the first live GitHub Pages site at https://origin4124.github.io/.
Replaced the original proof page with a more real Thriving Dancer landing page.
Added a studio scheduling / management concept section to the public site.
Verified the GitHub Pages update after propagation and closed the publish loop.
Sent Telegram notification with the live URL once the site was ready.
Established direct email communication with Trista.

Captured a strong first signal set from Trista: credentials, performance history, teaching background, founder role, age 3–14 mission focus, growth mindset, developmentally appropriate training, and whole-person philosophy.

Captured clearer founder answers: first audience = pre-professional dancers ages 10–16, first launch = podcast + blog, early products = workbook + ballet unit study, and the studio software lane is both internal and potentially product-facing later.
Set up a 1-hour autonomous Thriving Dancer assessment heartbeat to decide and take the next bounded project step.
Built and published a live Thriving Dancer project dashboard at /dashboard.html.
Updated the homepage and dashboard gates to minimal text only.

Created a working discovery brief, Planning Pack v1, website/content architecture draft, homepage copy draft, about-page outline, hero/CTA options, Start Here hub structure, first-visit flow draft, and first public launch checklist inside the project folder.

blockers.label: Dashboard/homepage gate propagation
blockers.detail: The homepage and dashboard both have the minimal passcode gate locally now, but public GitHub Pages propagation may lag briefly after updates.

blockers.label: Outlook reliability
blockers.detail: Outlook Web has been flaky under network drops, and replies can fall into draft state instead of sending unless verified carefully.

blockers.label: Native channel runtime
blockers.detail: Native Telegram/Discord channel registration in OpenClaw is still broken, so alerts and messaging depend on workarounds.

telegram.event: Thriving Dancer site became review-ready and complete.
Dashboard passphrase/project-log update finished locally and Outlook was cleaned down to one main tab.
Homepage proof-of-concept was updated to use the same light passphrase gate as the dashboard.

notifications.reviewReadyPolicy: Ping Kirill on Telegram when review is ready and again when the full loop is complete for important operations.
notifications.taskCompletionPreference: Ping Kirill on Telegram every time a task is finished.
notifications.githubVisibilityRule: When GitHub-hosted pages change in a way that gives Kirill something new to see, send a concise message with the relevant link after the change is live or reasonably visible.

automation.summary: Current autonomous activity and heartbeat behavior that keeps the project and ops surfaces moving.

items.name: Thriving Dancer project heartbeat
items.purpose: Assess project state, choose the most useful bounded next step, and make concrete progress.
currentBehavior: Reassess project state instead of following a hard-coded task list.
Prefer one bounded useful step per pass.
Keep dashboard data current when project path changes.
If heartbeat-critical work interrupts a task, check afterward and retry the interrupted task if still safe and useful.

items.name: Operation recovery sweep
items.purpose: Reconcile unfinished safe operations until they reach a terminal state.
currentBehavior: Retry safe work only when verification is inconclusive and retry is safe.
Escalate to needs_review instead of looping forever when risk or repeated failure appears.

items.name: Telegram fallback bridge
items.interval: Checked on heartbeat when at least 1 hour has passed since last Telegram check
items.purpose: Catch new human DMs while native messaging remains broken.
currentBehavior: Compare Telegram updates against last handled update id.
Reply briefly via direct Telegram Bot API when a new DM appears.
Close temporary Telegram tabs after use.

items.name: Outlook email watcher
items.interval: Checked on heartbeat when at least 1 hour has passed since last email check, except quiet hours
items.purpose: Surface relevant unread work/business email without babysitting the inbox.
currentBehavior: Quiet hours: skip email checks from 11:00 PM to 10:00 AM local time.
Escalate meaningful ambiguities when human judgment is needed.

items.name: Browser tab maintenance
items.interval: Applied during heartbeat and overnight work
items.purpose: Keep one necessary tab per recurring site unless multiple tabs are actively required.
currentBehavior: Close extra Outlook, GitHub, Telegram API, and other recurring tabs.
Keep a single useful tab open when possible.
Prefer a clean working browser state over tab buildup.

items: Set Thriving Dancer as a staged product direction and agreed on discovery first, website first, app later.
Established preference for PM/dev/QA style workflow once implementation ramps up.
Defined a stronger mission around building the business before the studio.
Reviewed Trista's LinkedIn and recognized a broader moat than just dance classes.
Built and published the first public Thriving Dancer landing page on GitHub Pages.
Started direct email communication with Trista and sent follow-up discovery questions.
Added autonomous project heartbeat for Thriving Dancer.
Built and published the project dashboard.
Created a working discovery brief from Trista's current signal.
Captured stronger founder answers on audience, launch channel, early products, urgency, and long-term software direction.
Added light passphrase gates to both the dashboard and homepage proof of concept.
Created Planning Pack v1, the website/content architecture draft, the homepage copy draft, and the Start Here hub structure.

Created the about-page outline, tightened hero/CTA options with a recommendation to use a combined Start Here destination first, defined the Start Here hub structure, created a first-visit flow draft, and made the public gates minimal-text only.
Improved heartbeat rules so interrupted tasks can be resumed after heartbeat-critical work finishes and so GitHub-visible updates trigger a message with a link.

projectLog.title: Set Thriving Dancer as a staged project
projectLog.detail: Agreed on discovery first, website first, and app/product ideas later.

projectLog.title: Clarified project mission
projectLog.detail: Reframed the work as building the business infrastructure, brand, offers, and systems before a physical studio exists.

projectLog.title: Published first public site
projectLog.detail: Created and deployed the first Thriving Dancer landing page on GitHub Pages.

projectLog.title: Started direct founder communication
projectLog.detail: Began email exchange with Trista and gathered initial bio, mission, and philosophy signal.

projectLog.title: Captured stronger founder answers
projectLog.detail: Confirmed first audience, first launch channel, early products, urgency, and long-term software direction.

projectLog.title: Dashboard and heartbeat became operating surfaces
projectLog.detail: The dashboard went live and the autonomous project heartbeat started driving next-step assessment.

projectLog.title: Dashboard hardening and cleanup
projectLog.detail: Added a light passphrase screen, richer project-log structure, task-finish notification note, and reduced Outlook to one main useful tab.

projectLog.title: Created Planning Pack v1
projectLog.detail: Turned the founder signal into positioning, audience, offer ladder, first launch channels, website structure, and strategic sequence.

projectLog.title: Created website/content architecture draft
projectLog.detail: Translated Planning Pack v1 into homepage goals, messaging hierarchy, page structure, content buckets, and CTA strategy.

projectLog.title: Created homepage copy draft
projectLog.detail: Turned the site architecture into a first pass of page copy for the homepage hero and main sections.

projectLog.title: Created about-page outline
projectLog.detail: Turned the founder story, credibility, philosophy, audience, and CTA into a page-ready narrative outline.

projectLog.title: Created hero and CTA options
projectLog.detail: Tightened the homepage top-of-page language, recommended a combined Start Here path, and set up the next step around a simple content hub.

projectLog.title: Created Start Here hub structure
projectLog.detail: Defined the first-click hub page so the homepage CTA can route dancers and parents into a simple guided content path.

projectLog.title: Created first-visit flow v1
projectLog.detail: Connected the homepage draft, About outline, hero options, and a 3-path Start Here block into a clearer first public visitor journey.

projectLog.title: Created first public launch checklist
projectLog.detail: Defined the lean first-release page set, CTA map, navigation, acceptance checklist, and recommendation to use the blog as the first live content destination.

projectLog.title: Added interruption-retry and GitHub visibility rules
projectLog.detail: Updated heartbeat guidance so interrupted bounded tasks can be retried after heartbeat-critical work and GitHub-visible updates should trigger a concise message with a link.
